attractions.name: Changing of the Guard at Buckingham Palace
attractions.description: This ceremony is a centuries-old military tradition embodying the continuity of the monarchy, discipline of the Foot Guards, and symbolic protection of the Sovereign & royal palaces.
attractions.hours: Mondays, Wednesdays, Fridays and Sundays at 11:00 am
source_files: England and Scotland/london.pdf

attractions.name: St. James's Park

attractions.name: Covent Garden
attractions.description: A vibrant cultural and shopping district in London's West End, renowned for its historic market, street performances, and proximity to world-class theatres

attractions.name: Chinatown London
attractions.description: Established in the 1950s, serves as a vibrant cultural enclave, offering a rich tapestry of East and Southeast Asian cuisine, festivals, and traditions in the heart of the West End
source_files: England and Scotland/london.pdf

attractions.name: West End Theatre
attractions.description: London's premier theatre district, renowned for its world-class productions, iconic venues, and rich theatrical history
attractions.hours: Evening shows typically start at 7:30 pm, matinees usually at 2:30 pm
attractions.entry_fee: Prices vary by show, The Phantom of the Opera starts at £32, Mamma Mia! begins at £19
